channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PTI Seeks Disqualification of Senator Saifullah Abro Over Voting Against Party Directive
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) chairman, Barrister Gohar Ali Khan, has formally submitted a reference to the Senate secretariat seeking the disqualification of Senator Saifullah Abro. T...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Doge Initiative: Attempting to Transform Government Through Technology and Gaming
item.description: In 2025, Elon Musk assumed a de facto leadership role in the US government through a department focused on efficiency, bringing a mindset heavily influenced by gaming, startup culture, and right-wing ...

item.title: Minor fire reported at Thiruvananthapuram Medical College hospital, patients safely evacuated
item.description: On the morning of March 17, 2026, a minor fire was reported at the surgical trauma care unit of the Government Medical College Hospital in Thiruvananthapuram at around 9:45 a.m. Fortunately, no casual...

item.title: Cross River Governor Orders Review of Sanitation Operations in Calabar Metropolis
item.description: Cross River State Governor, Bassey Otu, don announce say e wan make dem immediately review how dem dey run sanitation operations for Calabar metropolis. According to im Chief Press Secretary, Linus Ob...

item.title: MILF suspends engagement with government until new peace panel chair is appointed
item.description: The Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) announced a temporary suspension of key negotiations with the Philippine national government, citing the absence of a full-fledged chairman for the Government ...

item.title: Why normalising the loss of human life in wars must stop
item.description: The article by Shimbo Pastory reflects on how modern society and the global media have grown disturbingly comfortable with wars that disregard human life. It argues that many people now treat wars lik...

item.title: Regional Conflicts Threaten Dubai's Role as a Financial Hub
item.description: Dubai, long considered a neutral and secure financial gateway in the Middle East, is facing unprecedented challenges due to the ongoing regional conflict. The recent incident involving the impact of a...

item.title: Chinese Debates Over Global Influence Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Amid the recent tensions in the Middle East, particularly involving Iran, China is quietly debating its role in shaping global affairs. While U.S. President Donald Trump called for Chinese naval suppo...

item.title: Jade tries to keep distance from Kiko and Edward in Hotel 88
item.description: In the latest developments of the TV series 'The Secrets of Hotel 88,' the character Jade has been intentionally avoiding interactions with Kiko and Edward. Fans of the show have noticed her cautious ...

item.title: A Century of Spaceflight: From Goddard’s First Rocket to Private Space Companies
item.description: On March 16, 1926, Robert H. Goddard launched the first liquid-fueled rocket in Massachusetts, marking the beginning of modern rocketry. Though the rocket flew only 42 seconds and reached a height of ...

item.title: Understanding Zimbabwe’s diaspora and its role in national resilience
item.description: For over thirty years, Zimbabweans living abroad have quietly underpinned the survival of households and communities back home, sending billions in remittances that sustain education, healthcare, and ...

item.title: UN urges global action against rising Islamophobia amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The United Nations observed the International Day to Combat Islamophobia on March 15, drawing global attention to increasing anti-Muslim hatred and discrimination. Pakistan, which proposed the resolut...

item.title: Young Graduate Builds First Home for Parents Through Online Business
item.description: Ozioma Maryann Nnaji, a 28-year-old university graduate, has inspired many after she built her parents their first-ever house using money earned from an online business. Instead of taking the traditio...

item.title: Bacterial Meningitis Outbreak at University of Kent Claims Two Lives, Expert Provides Guidance
item.description: An outbreak of invasive bacterial meningitis at the University of Kent has resulted in two fatalities and left 11 students seriously ill, prompting the UK Health Security Agency to administer antibiot...

item.title: President Tinubu and First Lady embark on official UK state visit
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and First Lady Oluremi Tinubu don begin one historic two-day State Visit to United Kingdom, at the invite of King Charles III and Queen Camilla. Dis na the first time in de...

item.title: Exploring Microgrids as a Solution for Diesel-Dependent Remote Communities
item.description: Remote Australian communities face unique challenges when it comes to energy supply, as many rely heavily on diesel for electricity. About 500,000 Australians live off-grid, and in regions like the No...

item.title: Lapu Lapu survivor criticizes ICBC and donation handling after tragic festival incident
item.description: Blaine Redlac, 29, a survivor of the tragic Lapu Lapu Festival crash in Vancouver, is voicing frustration with British Columbia’s insurance and support systems almost a year after the incident. Redlac...

item.title: U.S. and Israeli Missile Interceptor Stocks Deplete Amid Ongoing Conflict with Iran
item.description: Israel has informed the United States that it is running low on ballistic missile interceptors as the war with Iran continues. The famed Iron Dome defense system, highly praised in the past, has faced...

item.title: RBA faces tough March interest rate decision amid inflation and global uncertainty
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) is grappling with one of its most complex interest rate decisions in years, influenced by rising inflation and global instability due to the war in the Middle East....

item.title: UDSM Opens New AI Centre to Boost Technology Education in Tanzania
